item.title: Cloud command queue has no replay protection — signed commands accepted without timestamp validation
item.intent: Prevent replay attacks on the cloud command queue. Every command must be validated for freshness before execution.
item.description: Code review found that src/cloud/commands/verify.ts verifies command signatures but never validates the createdAt timestamp against current time. A valid intercepted command can be replayed indefinitely. Additionally, the command queue in queue.ts marks commands as executed without actually dispatching to handlers.
steps: Add max-age validation to verifyCommandSignature() using createdAt timestamp
Add nonce tracking to detect exact replays within the validity window
Wire command queue to actual handler dispatch or document integration pattern
Add tests for expired commands and replayed commands
acceptanceCriteria: Command verification rejects commands older than a configurable max age (e.g. 5 minutes)
Replayed commands are detected and rejected with a clear error
Command queue either dispatches to actual handlers or documents the integration pattern
